description: Receipt verification MUST fail when any signed receipt field is tampered (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8 step 1). This fixture takes the rcpt-001 golden receipt and backdates created_at by one day while keeping the original signature: the recomputed receipt preimage diverges, so the signature no longer verifies. Backdating-after-mint is exactly the registry dishonesty receipts exist to make detectable (RFC-ACDP-0008 §9.1).

input.scenario: Consumer verifies a receipt whose created_at was altered after minting (original: 2026-04-16T10:30:15.123Z)

expected.behavior: The verifier JCS-recomputes the receipt preimage and obtains tampered_preimage_hash, not the original_preimage_hash the signature was produced over; Ed25519 verification of signature.value against the ASCII bytes of tampered_preimage_hash MUST fail. The verifier MUST report the receipt as failed (invalid_receipt category). Additionally, §8 step 3 independently catches the tamper: receipt.created_at no longer equals body.created_at.
expected.body_verdict: The accompanying body's producer signature and content_hash are unaffected — the body remains producer-authentic; only the registry's identifier/time binding is lost (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8).
